milestones.title: Murdoch's Connection footbridge opens
milestones.description: 47m-tall pedestrian and cycle bridge over the A63 linking the city centre to the marina.

milestones.title: Porter Street footbridge opens
milestones.description: Second active-travel bridge over the A63, west of Mytongate.

milestones.title: Completion delayed by a year
milestones.description: National Highways announced a c.12-month delay due to challenging ground conditions near the Humber.

milestones.title: Mytongate underpass opens to traffic
milestones.description: The 400m grade-separated A63 underpass opened; final landscaping continuing into spring 2026.

TR010037.name: A47 - A11 Thickthorn Junction
TR010037.summary: National Highways scheme to remodel the A47/A11 Thickthorn interchange south of Norwich with two free-flow underpass slip roads, one of four parallel A47 corridor NSIPs under RIS1/RIS2. DCO granted October 2022; survived the Andrew Boswell judicial review on cumulative climate grounds at the High Court (July 2023), Court of Appeal (Feb 2024) and Supreme Court (May 2024) — a leading authority on the limits of cumulative carbon assessment. After a funding hold and contractor change, ministers reapproved the £200m project in February 2025 and Skanska was appointed in July 2025.

milestones.title: Included in Road Investment Strategy 1
milestones.description: Thickthorn upgrade identified as part of the A47 corridor improvement programme under RIS1.

milestones.title: Non-statutory options consultation
milestones.description: Highways England held an initial public consultation in March 2017.

milestones.title: Preferred route announced
milestones.description: Free-flow underpass option selected for further development.

milestones.title: Statutory consultation
milestones.description: Statutory pre-application consultation 3 June - 11 July 2019.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: National Highways submitted the DCO; accepted for examination 28 April 2021.

milestones.title: Examination opens
milestones.description: Six-month examination Sep 2021 - Mar 2022.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: SoS granted the DCO authorising construction of the new underpasses.

milestones.title: Boswell High Court hearing
milestones.description: Joined judicial review of three A47 DCOs heard at the High Court on cumulative carbon emissions grounds.

milestones.title: High Court dismisses JR
milestones.description: Mrs Justice Thornton dismissed the challenge.

milestones.title: Galliford Try steps aside
milestones.description: National Highways retendered the £100m construction contract after Galliford Try withdrew.

milestones.title: Court of Appeal dismisses appeal
milestones.description: Cumulative carbon assessment ruled 'scientifically pointless' given emissions have no geographical boundary.

milestones.title: Supreme Court refuses permission
milestones.description: Domestic legal avenues exhausted; DCO confirmed.

milestones.title: Ministers reapprove £200m funding
milestones.description: Final funding go-ahead, citing 44,000 homes and 33,000 jobs in Greater Norwich City Deal.

milestones.title: Skanska awarded £153m contract
milestones.description: Enabling and advance works began on site.

milestones.title: Main construction begins
milestones.description: Main construction of the two underpasses scheduled to start in Q3 of FY 2025-26.

milestones.title: Scheme open to traffic (expected)
milestones.description: Programmed to open to traffic in early FY 2028-29.

EN010001.name: Hinkley Point C New Nuclear Power Station
EN010001.summary: Hinkley Point C was first proposed by the CEGB in 1982 as part of a UK PWR programme; it secured planning consent in 1990 after a 14-month public inquiry but was abandoned in 1995 when the privatised electricity market would not finance it. Revived by the 2008 nuclear White Paper, EDF took the project through a 2013 Development Consent Order and 2016 Final Investment Decision; construction is now under way with first power targeted for 2030 and total cost projected at around £46bn (2024 prices).

milestones.title: CEGB announces Hinkley Point C
milestones.description: Central Electricity Generating Board announces plans for a third station at Hinkley Point as part of a UK PWR programme alongside Sizewell B.

milestones.title: CEGB submits planning application
milestones.description: CEGB lodges a formal planning application for a 1,200 MW PWR station with West Somerset District Council.

milestones.title: Barnes Inquiry opens
milestones.description: 14-month public inquiry opens at Cannington, hearing around 600 witnesses and considering some 22,000 objections.

milestones.title: Inquiry recommends approval; consent granted
milestones.description: Michael Barnes QC's 3,300-page report recommends consent for the station; planning permission subsequently granted.

milestones.title: Nuclear stripped from privatisation
milestones.description: Electricity privatisation proceeds without nuclear assets; new PWR build is left without low-cost government finance.

milestones.title: Hinkley Point C abandoned
milestones.description: Following the 1994-95 government nuclear review, ministers conclude the private sector will not finance new nuclear and the project is shelved.

milestones.title: Nuclear White Paper
milestones.description: Brown government publishes 'Meeting the Energy Challenge', concluding nuclear should play a role and inviting private investment.

milestones.title: EDF acquires British Energy
milestones.description: EDF completes its £12.5bn takeover of British Energy, gaining the Hinkley Point site.

milestones.title: Hinkley Point nominated
milestones.description: Government lists Hinkley Point among eight sites considered suitable for new nuclear under the National Policy Statement process.

milestones.title: DCO application submitted
milestones.description: NNB Generation Company (EDF subsidiary) submits its DCO application to the Infrastructure Planning Commission.

milestones.title: Development Consent Order granted
milestones.description: Energy Secretary Ed Davey grants the DCO authorising EDF to build and operate Hinkley Point C.

milestones.title: Strike price agreed
milestones.description: Government and EDF agree a Contract for Difference at £92.50/MWh (2012 prices), index-linked, for 35 years from commissioning.

milestones.title: EDF Final Investment Decision
milestones.description: EDF's board approves FID with CGN as 33.5% partner; UK government approval follows in September.

milestones.title: First nuclear concrete poured
milestones.description: EDF pours the first nuclear-grade concrete for the Unit 1 common raft, formally starting nuclear island construction.

milestones.title: Cost rises to £22-23bn
milestones.description: EDF reports the project budget has risen to £21.5-22.5bn (2015 prices) with a 15-month delay risk.

milestones.title: Cost up to £46bn, start delayed to 2029-31
milestones.description: EDF announces the project will cost up to £46bn in 2024 prices, with Unit 1 start-up now expected between 2029 and 2031.

milestones.title: First power slips to 2030
milestones.description: EDF confirms electromechanical work has pushed Unit 1 first power back to 2030; outturn cost rising to about £35bn (2015 prices).

milestones.title: Unit 1 first power (target)
milestones.description: EDF's current schedule targets first electricity from Unit 1 in 2030.

milestones.title: Unit 2 first power (target)
milestones.description: Unit 2 scheduled to come online approximately a year after Unit 1, completing the 3.2 GW twin-EPR station.
